text: The surgeon creates enterotomies while controlling both edges with previously placed Vicryl stay sutures. The stapler is inserted, and the surgeon dynamically aligns each corner onto the stapler, ensuring control over the staple anastomosis. Both ends of the staple line are visible, and the surgeon hooks and dunks the staple line under to secure the corner.

The surgeon cuts off both ends of the previous sutures and performs a Lembert stitch, placing one stitch behind and then running a Lembert suture below. The needle is used to set the suture with proper tension, pulling the stay suture up and securing it with stitches in front and behind.
